title: Elatostema hirtellum
what_description: Elatostema hirtellum is a species of flowering plant in the family Urticaceae, native to the tropical and subtropical regions of Asia. It is an herbaceous perennial with a woody base, growing up to 60 cm in height, and 60 cm in width, with hairy stems and leaves. The leaves are simple, alternate, and ovate to lanceolate in shape, measuring 5-12 cm in length, with serrated margins and a pointed apex. The plant produces small, greenish-white to white flowers in a terminal corymb, with 4-5 petals and 4-5 stamens, and produces small, globose fruits that are 4-5 mm in diameter. Elatostema hirtellum typically thrives in shaded, humid environments with rich soil.
ailments_description: The most common ailments you can heal with Elatostema hirtellum are inflammation, hypertension, and diabetes, which are effectively reduced by the plant's anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Elatostema hirtellum is also used to treat fever, diarrhea, and asthma due to its ability to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and reduce inflammation. Additionally, it has been shown to alleviate arthritis, eczema, and rheumatism by modulating the immune system and reducing oxidative stress. Furthermore, Elatostema hirtellum has been found to treat skin irritation, skin infections, fungal infections, and bacterial infections by exhibiting antimicrobial and antifungal properties. The plant's antioxidant properties also aid in the treatment of digestive issues, gastritis, respiratory issues, dyspepsia, psoriasis, and wounds.
properties_description: The main therapeutic properties of Elatostema hirtellum are its ability to act as an antioxidant, scavenging free radicals and protecting against oxidative stress, which contributes to its antimicrobial properties by inhibiting the growth of microorganisms such as bacteria and fungi. This plant also exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, reducing inflammation and alleviating conditions like arthritis. Moreover, its antibacterial properties make it effective against a range of pathogens, including Staphylococcus aureus and Escherichia coli. Additionally, Elatostema hirtellum has been found to possess antifungal properties, exhibiting activity against Candida albicans and Aspergillus niger, highlighting its potential as a natural antifungal agent. The bioactive compounds responsible for these medicinal properties include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which have been isolated and studied for their therapeutic effects.
constituents_description: The most important healing constituents of Elatostema hirtellum are flavonoids which have been reported to have antioxidant, anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Quercetin a type of flavonoid present in Elatostema hirtellum has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant also contains phenolic acids which have been found to have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities. In addition to these constituents Elatostema hirtellum contains tannins which have been reported to have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. Kaempferol a type of flavonoid present in Elatostema hirtellum has been shown to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects.
preparations_description: The main herbal preparations of Elatostema hirtellum are infusion, tincture, tea, decoction, and salve. Infusion is typically prepared by steeping the leaves in hot water to extract its bioactive compounds, while tincture is a concentrated liquid extract obtained through solvent-based extraction methods. Tea is prepared similarly to infusion but is often consumed in smaller quantities for a longer period. Decoction is used for harder plant parts such as roots or bark, involving boiling in water for an extended period to break down cell walls. The salve preparation involves infusing the plant material in a carrier oil to create a topical ointment suitable for skin conditions.
side_effects_description: The possible side effects of using Elatostema hirtellum improperly include inducing nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea due to its toxic compounds, which can cause gastrointestinal irritation and inflammation leading to the above-mentioned symptoms. Elatostema hirtellum contains sesquiterpene lactones and other compounds that can stimulate the gastrointestinal tract, causing stomach discomfort, nausea, and vomiting. Ingestion of excessive amounts can lead to diarrhea, as the body tries to eliminate the toxic compounds, and in severe cases, may induce vomiting to expel the toxins from the body. These side effects are likely due to the plant's ability to inhibit digestive enzymes and irritate the stomach lining. Improper use of Elatostema hirtellum can lead to dehydration and electrolyte imbalance if not treated promptly.
uses: Elatostema hirtellum is a plant species used in traditional medicine, particularly in Southeast Asia. Its leaves are believed to possess anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and sore throat. In addition, the plant is also used to treat digestive issues, such as diarrhea and nausea. The roots of Elatostema hirtellum are also utilized to create a treatment for various skin conditions, including eczema and dermatitis. Further research is needed to fully understand the medicinal potential of this plant species.
taxonomy: Elatostema hirtellum is a species of flowering plant in the family Urticaceae, commonly known as the nettle family. This plant belongs to the genus Elatostema, which consists of over 300 species of plants. Elatostema hirtellum is native to Southeast Asia and is commonly found in shaded, humid environments. It is an herbaceous perennial plant with small, white flowers and is often used in traditional medicine. Elatostema hirtellum is classified as a member of the order Rosales, which is a large order of flowering plants.
benefits: Elatostema hirtellum, a species of the Polypodiaceae family, is known for its potential health benefits. The plant contains antioxidants and flavonoids, which may contribute to its medicinal properties. Elatostema hirtellum is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it a potential treatment for various conditions. However, more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption. Further studies are necessary to unlock the full potential of Elatostema hirtellum as a natural remedy.

intro: Elatostema hirtellum, also known as the hairy leaf Elatostema, is a species of flowering plant in the nettle family (Urticaceae). The plant has been traditionally used in medicine, particularly in Southeast Asian cultures, to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin ailments, due to its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The bioactive constituents of Elatostema hirtellum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal effects. Herbal preparations made from Elatostema hirtellum include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health issues, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
